section.heading: Enforcement by the Attorney General

subsection.heading: Civil action
subsection.chapeau: The Attorney General may commence a civil action in any appropriate district court of the United States against any person who—

paragraph.content: engages in a pattern or practice of violating this chapter; or
engages in a violation of this chapter that raises an issue of significant public importance.

subsection.heading: Relief
subsection.chapeau: In a civil action commenced under subsection (a), the court may—

paragraph.content: grant any appropriate equitable or declaratory relief with respect to the violation of this chapter;
award all other appropriate relief, including monetary damages, to any person aggrieved by the violation; and

paragraph.chapeau: may, to vindicate the public interest, assess a civil penalty—

subparagraph.content: in an amount not exceeding $55,000 for a first violation; and
in an amount not exceeding $110,000 for any subsequent violation.

content.p: Upon timely application, a person aggrieved by a violation of this chapter with respect to which the civil action is commenced may intervene in such action, and may obtain such appropriate relief as the person could obtain in a civil action under section 4042 of this title with respect to that violation, along with costs and a reasonable attorney fee.

section.sourceCredit: (Oct. 17, 1940, ch. 888, title VIII, § 801, as added Pub. L. 111–275, title III, § 303(a), Oct. 13, 2010, 124 Stat. 2877.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 597 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.
